figures.caption: Figure 2—figure supplement 2. Average saccades onset relative to the response cue onset in valid and neutral conditions (valid vs. neutral: p < 0.01). Only saccades that occurred after fixation period were included in the analysis. The trials presented here were not included in main analyses examining the effects of attention. Lines represent individual observers. Error bars mark group SEM.

Figure 4—figure supplement 1. Maximum a posteriori (MAP) estimates (points) and 95% confidence intervals for contrast sensitivity (CS) and asymptotic performance (AP) for each experimental condition across seven observers. Both CS and AP were transformed in the same way as used in our mixed-effects analyses.

Table 1. Average number and percentage of trials retained for analysis after filtering (mean ± SE acrossobservers) across different spatial frequencies and cueing conditions. Brackets indicate bootstrapped 95%confidence intervals. Observers who were unable to perform the task at 20 CPD were excluded from the 20CPD trial counts.
